data.title: QuantumScape Stock Falls 20% In 5 Trading Sessions: What's Going On?
data.description:
• QuantumScape shares are under pressure. What’s driving QS stock lower?

QuantumScape Q4 Loss Narrows

QuantumScape reported a fourth-quarter loss of 17 cents per share, which was better than the expected loss of 18 cents per share. The company also highlighted capital expenditures totaling $36.3 million for the full year, with customer billings reaching $19.5 million in 2025.

The company emphasized its diverse customer base and robust partner ecosystem, stating, “We believe we have a diverse group of customer and application opportunities, a robust and growing partner ecosystem, and a differentiated technology platform.”

Additionally, QuantumScape plans to focus on demonstrating scalable production of its solid-state battery technology in 2026.

Significant Short-Term Technical Weakness

The stock is currently trading 22.2% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 42.9% below its 100-day SMA, indicating significant weakness in the short to medium term.

Shares have decreased 28.34% over the past 12 months and are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, suggesting a challenging market environment.

The RSI is at 29.69, indicating that the stock is in oversold territory, which typically suggests a potential for a rebound. Meanwhile, the MACD shows a value of -0.8096, below its signal line at -0.7029, indicating bearish momentum.

The combination of oversold RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while the stock is oversold, there is still downward pressure.

  • Key Resistance: $7.50

QuantumScape’s Business Model

QuantumScape is engaged in the development of next-generation solid-state lithium-metal batteries for use in electric vehicles and other applications.

The company’s solid-state lithium-metal battery technology is designed to offer greater energy density, faster charging, and enhanced safety. Its battery cells have none of the host materials used in conventional anodes.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for QuantumScape, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Strong (Score: 69.62) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: QuantumScape’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum score, indicating positive price action relative to the market. While the stock faces technical challenges, the momentum score suggests potential for recovery as it navigates these hurdles.

QS Shares Edge Lower Wednesday

QS Price Action: QuantumScape shares were down 0.56% at $7.15 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Cadence Design Systems Posts Q4 Beat; Analysts See AI As Tailwind, Not Threat
data.description:
Here are the key analyst insights:

Check out other analyst stock ratings.

Rosenblatt Securities: Cadence Design Systems reported revenues of $1.44 billion. That’s up around 6% year-on-year and beating consensus of $1.424 billion, due to strength in Core EDA demand, Abernethy said. He added that the upside was driven by:

  • Continued stable contribution in China
  • Upfront revenue contributing 21% of total revenue, versus 18% in the third quarter,
  • Continued uptake of value-adding AI tools

AI is a tailwind rather than a threat for EDA, the analyst stated. The Hexagon simulation acquisition, which is expected to close in the coming weeks, "is not yet in the forecasts," he further wrote.

Needham: Cadence Design System sreported "another clean beat," with both fourth-quarter results and first-quarter guidance exceeding consensus estimates, Shi said. The company exited the quarter with $7.8 billion in backlog, implying net bookings of $2.2 billion, he added.

Management guided to 2026 revenue of $5.9-$6.0 billion, representing 11.4%-13.3% growth, the analyst stated. "We expect CDNS will likely deliver beat and raise through the year," he further wrote.

CDNS Price Action: Shares of Cadence Design Systems had risen by 9.48% to $310.32 at the time of publication on Wednesday.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Sensei Biotherapeutics Stock Soars 230% After Faeth Deal
data.description:
Sensei Biotherapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ:SNSE) surged on Wednesday following the company’s announcement of its acquisition of Faeth Therapeutics Inc. and a concurrent $200 million private investment.

The acquisition of Faeth Therapeutics brings its lead asset, PIKTOR, in development across solid tumor settings, into Sensei’s pipeline, which is an investigational all-oral combination targeting the PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway.

Private Placement

Concurrently, the company has secured a definitive agreement for a private placement financing expected to yield approximately $200 million, aimed at advancing PIKTOR through key clinical milestones, including a Phase 2 trial in advanced endometrial cancer.

Sensei expects to use the proceeds primarily to advance PIKTOR, as well as the initiation of a Phase 1b trial in HR+/HER2- advanced breast cancer, both expected by year-end 2026, with remaining proceeds for general corporate purposes and completion of Sensei’s ongoing Phase 1/2 trial of solnerstotug.

Technical Analysis

Sensei Biotherapeutics is currently trading 0.6% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 2.5% below its 100-day SMA, reflecting some short-term weakness. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 2.91%, and they are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, suggesting ongoing challenges in gaining upward momentum.

The RSI stands at 45.95, indicating neutral territory, while the MACD is below its signal line, suggesting bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD indicates mixed momentum, reflecting uncertainty in the stock’s near-term direction.

  • Key Resistance: $9.50
  • Key Support: $8.50

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $181.11. Recent analyst moves include:

  • HC Wainwright & Co.: Downgraded to Neutral (Oct. 30, 2025)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Sensei Biotherapeutics, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Neutral (Score: 50.4) — The stock is performing in line with the broader market.

The Verdict: Sensei Biotherapeutics’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed performance outlook. While the momentum score indicates a neutral stance, the recent acquisition may provide a necessary boost to its growth trajectory.

SNSE Price Action: Sensei Biotherapeutics shares were up 229.90% at $30.12 at the time of publication on Wednesday. The stock is trading at a new 52-week high, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock
